title: MEA Dos and Don'ts for Foreign Travel
citation: Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India, Dos and Don'ts for Foreign Travel
text: Source: Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India, Dos and Don'ts for Foreign Travel. MEA guidance says travellers should check the latest travel advisories of MEA and follow them. It says travellers should make sure they have the right visas for the countries they are visiting or transiting and check any other entry or exit requirements. It says travellers should obey the laws of the country they are visiting and should not expect to be treated differently from locals. It also advises appropriate travel and medical insurance, passport protection, at least six months passport validity from the planned return to India, copies of passports and visas, and registration with the local Indian Embassy or Consulate for better access to consular assistance and updates during longer stays.
